dependencies: Corn and soybean meal represented roughly 56% of cost of growing a live chicken domestically in fiscal 2024
Live cattle supply from independent feed yards and public auctions
Contract farmers for raising live chickens
risks: Fluctuations in commodity prices for corn, soybean meal, live cattle, live hogs
Limited or reduced domestic availability of live cattle
Periods of imbalance in supply and demand for livestock
Disease outbreaks (African swine fever, avian influenza, BSE) affecting livestock supply
Labor shortages and increased turnover impacting production capacity
Dependence on independent contract farmers and producers

regulatory_risks: USDA and FDA inspection and food safety regulations
HACCP program compliance
Sanitation Standard Operating Procedures
Public Health Security and Bioterrorism Preparedness and Response Act of 2002
Environmental laws and regulations (air, land, water protection)
Greenhouse gas emission controls and reporting requirements
Climate-related disclosure requirements (evolving)
OSHA workplace safety regulations
Labor laws and collective bargaining requirements
Antitrust laws and investigations (DOJ, state attorneys general)
Grain Inspection, Packers and Stockyards Act compliance
Competition Act (Canada)
International food safety and quality standards
Trade restrictions and import/export regulations
Animal disease control regulations
Data privacy regulations (EU and other jurisdictions)
Tax law changes and compliance (federal, state, foreign jurisdictions)
Advertising and marketing restrictions
Product labeling and disclosure requirements
Packaging specifications and waste reduction mandates
Extended producer responsibility obligations
Single-use plastics regulations

sanction_risks: Conflicts between Ukraine and Russia with potential for escalation and additional economic sanctions
Middle East conflicts with risks outside company control
Potential border closures by foreign countries due to animal disease or health issues
